operating_state: Third quarter 2024 earnings of $8.6 billion decreased from $9.1 billion in the prior year, driven by weaker industry refining margins partially offset by record Permian production including the Pioneer acquisition and higher Guyana volumes. Upstream production reached 4.6 million oil-equivalent barrels per day, up 894 thousand barrels from the prior year, with advantaged volume growth contributing earnings benefits. Energy Products margins declined significantly due to industry refining conditions at the low end of the 10-year range, while Chemical Products margins improved slightly from lower feed costs.
cost_structure: Structural cost savings delivered quarterly earnings benefits across segments, bringing cumulative savings to $11.3 billion versus 2019 levels. Total cash operating expenses excluding energy and production taxes increased year-to-date driven by inflation, higher activity levels from acquisitions, increased Upstream depreciation, and planned maintenance activities.
material_events: ExxonMobil completed the $60 billion Pioneer Natural Resources acquisition on May 3, 2024, adding significant Permian Basin production capacity. The company distributed dividends and repurchased common stock totaling $26.1 billion during the first nine months. Gregory J. Goff resigned from the board of directors on October 17, 2024, and ExxonMobil issued floating rate notes due 2074 in October 2024.
macro_exposures: Commodity price volatility remains the primary earnings driver with oil prices in the middle of the 10-year range while natural gas strengthened toward the top half. Industry refining margins compressed to the low end of historical ranges due to oversupply conditions. Chemical margins remained well below the 10-year range driven by oversupply in Asia and bottom-of-cycle industry conditions.
forward_outlook: Capital and exploration expenditures are anticipated at approximately $28 billion for 2024, with continued focus on advantaged assets including Permian, Guyana, Brazil, and LNG projects. The company maintains capital allocation priorities of investing in high-return projects, maintaining balance sheet strength, and consistent shareholder returns through dividends and share repurchases. Strategic projects under development include renewable diesel facilities and chemical complexes.
uncertainty_profile: Business results remain subject to commodity price fluctuations, economic conditions, and supply-demand dynamics across global energy markets. Lower-emission business opportunities depend on market development, supportive government policies, and technological advancement for commercial-scale cost-effectiveness. The company faces ongoing regulatory and policy risks including potential changes in tax regimes, environmental regulations, and trade sanctions that could impact operations and financial performance.
